questions.prompt: Which of these is the BIGGEST single optimization for reducing OpenAI API costs in a chat product?

choices.text: Implementing conversation compaction (summarize old turns) and prompt caching (stable content at the start of prompts so OpenAI's prefix cache kicks in). Often 70–90% cost reduction combined.
Switching from JSON to plain text output.
Adding more validation to user input.

questions.explanation: Conversation history is the #1 cost growth pattern — without compaction, every turn re-sends the full history, so cost grows linearly with conversation length. Prompt caching gives ~50% off on stable prefixes that repeat across requests. Together they routinely cut chat product costs 70–90%.

questions.prompt: You're about to ship a feature that will make ~100,000 OpenAI calls per month. What's the right pre-launch move?

choices.text: Just ship and monitor — fix cost issues if they emerge.

Estimate cost per request before launch: count expected input tokens (system prompt + context + user input + tools), expected output tokens, multiply by per-token rates for the chosen model, multiply by request volume. If the projection is uncomfortable, audit prompt size, drop to a smaller model, or apply optimizations BEFORE shipping.
Always switch to the cheapest model regardless of quality.
Default to gpt-5 'just in case' — quality first.

questions.explanation: Cost estimation before launch is the most leverage you'll have. 100K calls/month at the wrong model or wrong prompt size can be a 50× difference in monthly bill. Use tiktoken locally to count tokens, plug in current per-token rates, multiply by volume. The 30 minutes saves real money.
